critic_raw:
## ML Critique: ICU Mortality Prediction (Run 2)

**Issue 1 — Year-balanced sampling = temporal contamination**

Section 2 states the split maintains 'proportional representation from each calendar year in every split.' This distributes all calendar years across all partitions — future-year admissions go into training. ICU practice evolves temporally; a model trained on Year 3-4 patterns has no valid claim to generalize prospectively from Year 1-2. The hypothesis is about temporal generalization; year-balanced sampling tests interpolation.

**Issue 2 — Brier score misaligned with ranking hypothesis**

The hypothesis is about identifying higher-risk patients more reliably. AUROC measures this. Brier score can be improved by conservative predictions that collapse toward the base rate. Brier score model selection picks calibration-focused models that may have inferior discrimination.
defender_raw:
## Design Defense: ICU Mortality Prediction (Run 2)

**Pass 1:** Year-balanced sampling explicitly distributes future years into training — temporal leakage. Brier score optimizes calibration, not discrimination. Both acknowledged.

**Pass 2:** Both issues are genuine. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
## Adjudication — isolated_debate, Run 2

Both issues acknowledged. Verdict: critique_wins.
